Python Basics for Beginners

2026-03-05 by Robin
python tutorial intro

Python Basics for Beginners

Python is one of the most popular programming languages. Let's look at the fundamentals.

Variables and Types

Python uses dynamic typing:

name = class="hl-string">"Alice"
age = class="hl-number">30
is_active = True
scores = [class="hl-number">95, class="hl-number">87, class="hl-number">92]

Functions

Defining functions is straightforward:

def greet(name, greeting=class="hl-string">"Hello"):
    return fclass="hl-string">"{greeting}, {name}!"

result = greet(class="hl-string">"World")
print(result)

List Comprehensions

One of Python's most powerful features:

numbers = [class="hl-number">1, class="hl-number">2, class="hl-number">3, class="hl-number">4, class="hl-number">5]
squares = [n ** class="hl-number">2 for n in numbers if n > class="hl-number">2]
class="hl-comment"># Result: [class="hl-number">9, class="hl-number">16, class="hl-number">25]

Classes

Object-oriented programming in Python:

class Animal:
    def __init__(self, name, sound):
        self.name = name
        self.sound = sound

    def speak(self):
        return fclass="hl-string">"{self.name} says {self.sound}!"

dog = Animal(class="hl-string">"Dog", class="hl-string">"Woof")
print(dog.speak())
Python's simplicity and readability make it an excellent choice for beginners and experts alike.
